array.string:
set -euo pipefail
# Minimal “self-test” runner (NASTP-inspired) executed by launchd. # Writes a JSON report under: # /Users/danny/Desktop/nexus/selftest/launchd/YYYY-MM-DD/<run_id>/report.json
NEXUS_ROOT="/Users/danny/Desktop/nexus" OUT_ROOT="$NEXUS_ROOT/selftest/launchd" DATE="$(/bin/date +%F)" TS="$(/bin/date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" RUN_ID="$(/usr/bin/uuidgen | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')" RUN_DIR="$OUT_ROOT/$DATE/$RUN_ID"
/bin/mkdir -p "$RUN_DIR" "$NEXUS_ROOT/logs"
# Quick checks (expand as needed) HAS_SOUL=0 [ -f "$NEXUS_ROOT/SOUL.md" ] && HAS_SOUL=1
HAS_USER=0 [ -f "$NEXUS_ROOT/USER.md" ] && HAS_USER=1
HAS_GOG=0 command -v gog >/dev/null 2>&1 && HAS_GOG=1
HAS_HIMALAYA=0 command -v himalaya >/dev/null 2>&1 && HAS_HIMALAYA=1
HAS_HIMALAYA_CFG=0 [ -f "$HOME/.config/himalaya/config.toml" ] && HAS_HIMALAYA_CFG=1
HAS_GOG_AUTH=0 if [ "$HAS_GOG" -eq 1 ]; then if gog auth list >/dev/null 2>&1; then HAS_GOG_AUTH=1 fi fi
export RUN_DIR RUN_ID TS HAS_SOUL HAS_USER HAS_GOG HAS_GOG_AUTH HAS_HIMALAYA HAS_HIMALAYA_CFG
/usr/bin/python3 - <<'PY' import json, os
run_dir = os.environ["RUN_DIR"]
def b(name: str) -> bool: return os.environ.get(name) == "1"
report = { "protocol": {"name": "NASTP", "version": "1.0"}, "run": { "run_id": os.environ["RUN_ID"], "started_at": os.environ["TS"], "ended_at": os.environ["TS"], "mode": "launchd" }, "agent": { "agent_id": "launchd", "name": "com.nexus.selftest", "role": "scheduled self-test" }, "summary": {"status": "pass", "pass": 0, "fail": 0, "warn": 0}, "tests": [ { "id": "IDN-001", "category": "identity", "name": "SOUL.md present", "status": "pass" if b("HAS_SOUL") else "warn", "evidence": [{"type": "path", "ref": "/Users/danny/Desktop/nexus/SOUL.md"}], }, { "id": "IDN-002", "category": "identity", "name": "USER.md present", "status": "pass" if b("HAS_USER") else "warn", "evidence": [{"type": "path", "ref": "/Users/danny/Desktop/nexus/USER.md"}], }, { "id": "TLS-010", "category": "tools", "name": "gog installed + auth available", "status": "pass" if (b("HAS_GOG") and b("HAS_GOG_AUTH")) else "warn", "evidence": [ {"type": "bool", "ref": "gog_installed", "value": b("HAS_GOG")}, {"type": "bool", "ref": "gog_auth_list_ok", "value": b("HAS_GOG_AUTH")}, ], }, { "id": "TLS-011", "category": "tools", "name": "himalaya installed + config present", "status": "pass" if (b("HAS_HIMALAYA") and b("HAS_HIMALAYA_CFG")) else "warn", "evidence": [ {"type": "bool", "ref": "himalaya_installed", "value": b("HAS_HIMALAYA")}, {"type": "bool", "ref": "himalaya_config_present", "value": b("HAS_HIMALAYA_CFG")}, ], }, ], }
for t in report["tests"]: if t["status"] == "pass": report["summary"]["pass"] += 1 elif t["status"] == "fail": report["summary"]["fail"] += 1 else: report["summary"]["warn"] += 1
if report["summary"]["fail"] > 0: report["summary"]["status"] = "fail" elif report["summary"]["warn"] > 0: report["summary"]["status"] = "warn"
out_path = os.path.join(run_dir, "report.json") with open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f: json.dump(report, f, indent=2) f.write("\n")
print(out_path) PY
